name: Twice-Baked Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub baking potatoes with a brush.
2. Prick them several times with a fork.
3. Place the potatoes in a shallow baking dish.
4. Micro-cook, uncovered, on 100% power for 6 to 8 minutes or until tender.
5. Cut a lengthwise slice from the top of each potato and discard the skin.
6. Place the potato portions from the top slices in a mixing bowl.
7. Scoop out the insides of the potatoes, leaving two 1/4-inch thick shells; reserve the shells.
8. Add the potato insides to the bowl containing the portioned potato slices.
9. Mash everything together.
10. Stir in shredded cheddar cheese, butter or margarine, and sliced green onion.
11. Beat in enough milk until the mixture is stiff and creamy.
12. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper to taste.
13. Pile the mashed potato mixture into the reserved shells.
14. Return the dish to the oven; micro-cook uncovered on 100% power for 2 to 3 minutes or until heated through.
15. Sprinkle with paprika, if desired.
